The characteristic waveform observed on duplex Doppler for veins in the lower limbs typically shows a triphasric pattern, reflecting the components from pre- and post-capillary segments. This includes a high velocity phase during systole (forward filling), followed by a low or absent diastolic flow component due to valve closure mechanisms.
